source.title: Ensemble-based enzyme design can recapitulate the effects of laboratory directed evolution in silico

initial_request.target_name: HG4 Kemp eliminase redesigned from an HG3/HG3.17 evolutionary trajectory
initial_request.input_data: HG-series Kemp eliminase lineage, room-temperature crystal structures with and without 6NT, HG3.17 mutation set near the active site, and computational enzyme-design models using fixed backbones or crystallographic ensemble refinement.
initial_request.user_intent: Understand which conformational changes produced the activity gains during laboratory evolution and use that knowledge to design a high-activity enzyme computationally, reducing the need for repeated wet-lab evolution.
initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Recover a Kemp eliminase with catalytic efficiency around 10^5 M^-1 s^-1, more than 700-fold above HG3 and close to HG3.17, while recapitulating the productive transition-state geometry using computational modeling.

evidence.support: The paper describes building HG4 from HG3 by introducing HG3.17 active-site and second-shell mutations to test whether computational design can approach the evolved activity.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: HG3.17 reached much higher catalytic efficiency than the original HG3 design after many directed-evolution mutations. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which mechanistic changes, rather than just sequence differences, explain the activity increase. [Decision] Audit the evolved trajectory and compare kinetics across HG-series variants to define the structural questions that need to be answered.
agent_trajectory.observation: The study established that HG3.17 was approximately three orders of magnitude better than HG3, motivating a structural analysis of the evolutionary path.

evidence.support: The paper states that mutations introduced into HG3 to produce HG3.17 increased catalytic efficiency by approximately three orders of magnitude.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Kinetic gains are established, but cryogenic structures may hide relevant conformational heterogeneity. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: How the accessible conformational ensemble changed during evolution. [Decision] Solve room-temperature crystal structures for all HG-series enzymes with and without bound 6NT to expose catalytically relevant heterogeneity.
agent_trajectory.observation: Room-temperature structures across the HG lineage revealed the bound pose and the conformational heterogeneity needed to analyze productive versus nonproductive substates.

evidence.support: The paper reports solving room-temperature structures of all HG-series Kemp eliminases in both ligand-bound and unbound states at 1.35-1.99 A resolution.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Structures across the trajectory are available. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which physical changes in the ensemble enabled higher catalysis. [Decision] Quantify rigidity, pre-organization, and active-site accessibility across the trajectory to identify the mutations and conformations that matter most.
agent_trajectory.observation: The analysis showed progressive rigidification near residue 50, widened access channels, and about 40% productive pre-organization in unbound HG3.17, providing a structural basis for the evolved activity gains.

evidence.support: The paper reports that HG3.17 has a large fraction of molecules pre-organized for catalysis and discusses loop rigidification and widened substrate access during evolution.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: The structural determinants of improved catalysis are clearer. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether these insights can be turned into a compact computational redesign. [Decision] Build HG4 by introducing eight first- and second-shell mutations from HG3.17 into HG3, focusing on positions near the transition state and at the channel entrance.
agent_trajectory.observation: HG4 was proposed as an eight-mutation redesign intended to capture the main catalytic improvements of HG3.17 without the full distal mutation set.

evidence.support: The paper states that HG4 includes HG3.17 mutations within 7.5 A of 6NT plus W275A and R276F to widen the active-site entrance.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: HG4 sequence has been defined. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Single fixed-backbone design may miss subtle but crucial conformational differences. [Decision] Compare HG4 modeling on the original 1GOR backbone, on HG3 crystal templates, and on HG3-derived ensemble-refinement templates to test which representation can recover the correct transition-state pose and energy.
agent_trajectory.observation: The 1GOR fixed backbone produced a model that differed substantially from the HG4 crystal structure and was destabilized by about 45 kcal/mol, while HG3-derived ensemble members recovered the correct transition-state binding mode with energies comparable to the HG4 crystal structure.

evidence.support: The paper reports that the 1GOR-derived model was destabilized by about 45 kcal/mol and that HG3-derived ensemble refinement recapitulated the correct binding mode with comparable energies.
The paper concludes that crystallographically derived HG3 ensembles can sample catalytically competent substates during design and may obviate additional directed evolution.

success_verification.validation_technique: Kinetic measurement of HG-series variants, room-temperature crystallography with and without 6NT, and computational comparison of fixed-backbone versus ensemble-refined design templates.

HG4 catalytic efficiency.interpretation: HG4 reached a catalytic efficiency of 103,000 M^-1 s^-1, placing it near the natural-enzyme range and close to the evolved HG3.17 benchmark.

Improvement over HG3.interpretation: HG4 was reported to be more than 700-fold more efficient than HG3.

Pre-organized HG3.17 molecules.interpretation: Approximately 40% of unbound HG3.17 molecules were already pre-organized for catalysis, explaining much of the evolutionary gain.

1GOR model destabilization.interpretation: Replacing the HG4 backbone context with the original 1GOR design template generated a substantially destabilized model, highlighting the need for ensemble-aware templates.

success_verification.final_verdict: The study successfully showed that ensemble-based computational design can capture key benefits of laboratory evolution: HG4 retained near-HG3.17 activity with only eight selected mutations, and the decisive modeling improvement came from using experimentally grounded backbone ensembles rather than a single fixed template.

evidence.support: The paper reports HG4 at 103,000 M^-1 s^-1 and HG3.17 at 126,000 M^-1 s^-1.
The paper states that HG4 is more than 700-fold better than HG3, equivalent to the average natural enzyme range, and only about 20% less active than HG3.17.

quality.notes: 基于 Nature Communications 的 CC-BY 开放论文人工整理，并保留页面级证据指针。
